text: The patient is positioned in the modified French position with dual lumen intubation. A varus needle is inserted in the right upper quadrant to insufflate the abdomen, avoiding the jejunostomy tube on the left. The falciform ligament is taken down, and a Nathanson retractor is inserted. Laparoscopic instruments are used to carefully reduce the herniated small bowel, revealing the majority of the small bowel within the hernia sac. Further reduction exposes the transverse colon within the hernia sac. The hernia sac and associated adhesions are transected to visualize the left and right diaphragmatic crura.

The surgeon assesses blood perfusion to the stomach using near-infrared technology. The right upper quadrant camera port is exchanged from a 5mm to an 8mm port, and the XI robot platform is docked. Abdominal pressure is decreased to facilitate primary hernia repair using interrupted sutures. The repair is performed from anterior to posterior to avoid manipulating the gastric conduit. The right cruce, noted to be weak from previous incision during esophagectomy, is included in the cruroplasty without mesh reinforcement.
